raw_consumer_statement:
In XXXX  someone used my identity to fraudulently apply for a Barclays XXXX XXXX. Barclays approved the application and the person proceeded to purchase items from an Old Navy store in Ohio. Total charges over {$360.00}. 

I received a delinquent payment letter in XXXX  and called Barclays to tell them I do not have a credit card with them. I reported this as fraud, and they said they would perform a thorough investigation. After investigating, they told me I was responsible. I was able to get a copy of the credit card application which had some accurate information but also had inaccurate information. 

Barclays said they would perform another thorough investigation. Soon I received another letter stating I was responsible for the charges. 

This has gone on for 2 years and multiple phones calls with Barclays. When I call Barclays I get routed to many departments ( Fraud, Collections, customer service, etc ) but it is still not resolved. 

I then sought the help of XXXX XXXX identify theft department. XXXX ). XXXX XXXX called me, and we discussed it and then had a conference call with Barclays. 

They have finally agreed that the application was fraudulent but that I still owe them the money! That doesn't make sense. 

I have filed a police report with the XXXX Texas Police department. The report number is XXXX. I have also reported this to the Consumer Protection agency of Texas.
